en: Fall protection measures must be provided where fall heights exceed one meter at walls, stairs, and machine platforms, and two meters at workplaces and traffic routes.\nConstruction Workers Protection Ordinance, Section 7(1), 7(2)3, 7(2)4\nWorkplace Ordinance, Section 11(3-4)

Collective protective measures such as guardrails or covers must be implemented where required. Personal fall protection equipment may only be used where collective protective measures are not possible or insufficient.\nConstruction Workers Protection Ordinance, Section 6(8)2, 7(4)

Openings in floors or similar surfaces must be secured by load-bearing covers, guardrails, or equivalent protective measures. Where covers are not possible, equivalent safeguarding or clear hazard marking must be provided.\nWorkplace Ordinance, Section 11(2)

Workplaces and traffic routes must be protected where persons may be endangered by falling objects. Suitable protective measures such as coverings, protective roofs, barriers, or restricted zones must be implemented.\nConstruction Workers Protection Ordinance, Section 6(1)

When working near unprotected edges, appropriate safety distances, barriers, or clearly marked and secured restricted zones must be established to prevent falls.\nConstruction Workers Protection Ordinance, Section 9(3)2
Entry into barrier zones must be limited to operational necessity.\nConstruction Workers Protection Ordinance, Section 9(4)
Restricted Access to Edge Protection Zones
